jokes.text: So it doesn't turn into another giant sausage fest.
jokes.setup: Jack explains needing women for a McCain fundraising dinner
jokes.punchline: The crude 'sausage fest' terminology from the corporate executive
jokes.explanation: Classic Jack - formal business talk with crude terminology. Well-crafted character moment.

jokes.text: What's the Committee to Reinvade Vietnam?
jokes.setup: Liz asking about the fundraising dinner organization
jokes.punchline: The absurd committee name suggesting re-invading Vietnam
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd committee name that's both specific and ridiculous. High quotability.

jokes.text: No, C. Nor and I had a falling-out after I switched to another dojo.
jokes.setup: Liz sarcastically asking if Chuck Norris is a cool Republican celebrity
jokes.punchline: Jack casually referring to Chuck Norris as 'C. Nor' and having dojo beef
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack absurdity - casual nickname for Chuck Norris plus dojo drama. Excellent character integration.

jokes.text: Also I'm saving that money for new humidifiers. The same model that's keeping Larry King alive.
jokes.setup: Liz explaining why she won't donate money
jokes.punchline: The specific detail about Larry King being kept alive by humidifiers
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Larry King's age/mortality with specific product detail makes it land well.

jokes.text: Food network doesn't have a news show.
jokes.setup: Jack asking if she watches the news about the subway hero
jokes.punchline: Liz's admission she only watches Food Network
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz character moment - food obsessed and out of touch. Maximum economy.

jokes.text: Thank you, mayor Bloomberger.
jokes.setup: Dennis accepting an award from the mayor
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing Bloomberg as 'Bloomberger'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis - close enough to be recognizable but wrong enough to show his stupidity.

jokes.text: Stern rules! Baba booey!
jokes.setup: Solemn award ceremony for Dennis
jokes.punchline: Someone yelling Howard Stern catchphrases during the ceremony
jokes.explanation: Classic disruption of formal ceremony trope. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: And Dateline predator. / Exonerated Dateline predator.
jokes.setup: Jack listing Dennis's credentials
jokes.punchline: Dennis's proud correction about being 'exonerated'
jokes.explanation: Dennis's pride in being 'exonerated' from predator charges is perfectly in character and disturbing.

jokes.text: Like the Giuliani campaign.
jokes.setup: Dennis calling his Dateline situation 'one big silly misunderstanding'
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to the Giuliani campaign
jokes.explanation: Topical political humor with good structure - unexpected comparison from Dennis.

jokes.text: when I say subway, you say hero. / - Subway. - Hero?
jokes.setup: Jack trying to do a call-and-response chant
jokes.punchline: Jonathan's confused, questioning delivery of 'Hero?'
jokes.explanation: Jonathan's confusion undermines Jack's attempt at a rallying cry. Good character work.

jokes.text: We're like Ross and Rachel, but just not gay.
jokes.setup: Dennis describing his relationship with Liz
jokes.punchline: Calling Ross and Rachel gay
jokes.explanation: Typical Dennis casual homophobia disguised as relationship comparison. Character-accurate but cheap humor.

jokes.text: She told me that her last boyfriend was Asian. And that crap doesn't start until college.
jokes.setup: Dennis defending knowing the girl was 18
jokes.punchline: His racist assumption about Asian boyfriends starting in college
jokes.explanation: Dennis being casually racist is in character but relies on offensive stereotypes for humor.

jokes.text: He was a major tv star in the '40s, '50s and the fall of 1972
jokes.setup: Jonathan introducing Bucky Bright as a major TV star
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific 'fall of 1972' addition
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specificity - implies his comeback was extremely brief. Perfect 30 Rock absurdist timing.

jokes.text: Son of a married couple, Bucky Bright!
jokes.setup: Kenneth recognizing the old TV star
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's wholesome alternative to 'son of a bitch'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth - wholesome exclamation that's somehow more memorable than swearing. Instant classic.

jokes.text: Choosing is a sin, so I always just write in the lord's name.
jokes.setup: Jack asking if Kenneth would vote Republican if Bucky told him to
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's explanation that choosing is sinful
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Kenneth logic - voting is choosing, choosing is sinful. Character-perfect theological loophole.

jokes.text: That's Republican-- we count those.
jokes.setup: Kenneth saying he writes in the Lord's name
jokes.punchline: Jack matter-of-factly claiming those votes for Republicans
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack opportunism - immediately claiming religious votes. Great deadpan delivery implied.

jokes.text: we didn't have any of those shirts with words on 'em, or dungarees, or anything like that
jokes.setup: Bucky describing the classy old days
jokes.punchline: Old man complaints about modern fashion
jokes.explanation: Standard old man character humor - predictable but fits the character.

jokes.text: And an inside monogrammed pocket, you know, for your opium pipe and your switchblade.
jokes.setup: Bucky describing elegant men's fashion from the past
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning pockets for opium pipes and switchblades
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from elegant fashion to casual drug paraphernalia. Perfect 'good old days' subversion.

jokes.text: 400% of my sodium? I should not be eating these.
jokes.setup: Liz looking at nutritional information while eating
jokes.punchline: Continuing to eat while acknowledging how bad they are
jokes.explanation: Classic Liz food contradiction - knows it's bad but can't stop. Character-accurate observational humor.

jokes.text: the bravest New Yorker since Bernie Goetz
jokes.setup: Jack introducing Dennis as a hero
jokes.punchline: Comparing him to Bernie Goetz, the subway vigilante
jokes.explanation: Dark reference to controversial subway vigilante. Jack would make this comparison unironically.

jokes.text: It's the Stanley cup, honey. It's hockey's ultimate prize. And me and it, we're teaming up to fight illiteracy.
jokes.setup: Liz asking about Dennis's trophy
jokes.punchline: Dennis claiming to have the Stanley Cup and using it to fight illiteracy
jokes.explanation: Absurd Dennis logic - stealing hockey's ultimate prize for literacy campaigns. Perfect character absurdity.

jokes.text: I've watched seven different people die in subway stations.
jokes.setup: Someone saying they wouldn't have the guts to do what Dennis did
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning watching seven people die in subways
jokes.explanation: Shockingly dark admission delivered matter-of-factly. Excellent dark comedy with perfect specificity.

jokes.text: All I could think about was Derek Jeter. And it was like, check this out, Jeter, you think you're better than me?
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining his heroic mindset
jokes.punchline: His motivation was proving something to Derek Jeter
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis - turning a heroic moment into petty competition with a baseball player. Brilliantly character-specific.

jokes.text: They should call this thing 'The Weeks.'
jokes.setup: Dennis complaining about The Hours movie feeling long
jokes.punchline: Suggesting it should be called 'The Weeks'
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about pretentious movies. Dennis delivering cultural critique is funny.

jokes.text: I only have Spanish delis in my neighborhood.
jokes.setup: Liz knowing the Spanish Cheetos name
jokes.punchline: Her matter-of-fact explanation about her neighborhood
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz - casual acceptance of her weird food situation. Character-specific urban detail.

jokes.text: Does hot support cold? Does rain support the earth?
jokes.setup: Tracy rejecting the idea of black Republicans
jokes.punchline: His pseudo-philosophical rhetorical questions
jokes.explanation: Tracy's faux-deep rhetorical questions are perfectly in character - sounds profound but makes no sense.

jokes.text: If I paid taxes, I sure would.
jokes.setup: Jack asking if Tracy likes lower taxes
jokes.punchline: Tracy admitting he doesn't pay taxes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy - casually admitting tax evasion. Maximum economy and character accuracy.

jokes.text: Lincoln was a Republican? / Actually, today's Republican party would be unrecognizable to Lincoln.
jokes.setup: Jack mentioning Lincoln as Republican, Tracy expressing surprise
jokes.punchline: Dotcom's detailed historical correction
jokes.explanation: Classic Dotcom - being the smartest guy in the room with historical accuracy. Good character contrast.

jokes.text: Dotcom, this need you have to be the smartest guy in the room is... off-putting. / I guess that's why I'm still single.
jokes.setup: Jack criticizing Dotcom's know-it-all tendency
jokes.punchline: Dotcom's sad self-awareness about being single
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Dotcom's self-awareness about his off-putting intelligence. Relatable cringe.

jokes.text: No, no, no, two guys'd get-- hey, my old dressing room.
jokes.setup: Kenneth asking if sandwich girls got sandwiches
jokes.punchline: Bucky starting to explain something sexual then getting distracted
jokes.explanation: Old Hollywood sleaze avoided by convenient distraction. The implication is clear without being explicit.

jokes.text: For five bucks I will.
jokes.setup: Kenneth asking about 'sandwich girls'
jokes.punchline: Bucky offers sexual services for money
jokes.explanation: Dark old man humor - casual prostitution offer. Short and shocking.

jokes.text: What, are you gearing up for swimsuit season?
jokes.setup: Dennis mocks Liz for ordering salad
jokes.punchline: Uses cheesy pickup line mockingly
jokes.explanation: Weak humor relying on fat-shaming stereotype. Character-appropriate for Dennis but not clever.

jokes.text: We used to call this 'the jew room.'
jokes.setup: Bucky showing Kenneth around the old studio
jokes.punchline: Casually dropping the antisemitic room name
jokes.explanation: Period-accurate casual antisemitism from the old TV star. Uncomfortable but character-consistent.

jokes.text: because this Indian guy that he was working for...
jokes.setup: Liz trying to explain Dennis's credit problems
jokes.punchline: Getting lost in an increasingly complex story
jokes.explanation: Liz getting trapped in explaining Dennis's convoluted problems. Character-accurate but not exceptional.

jokes.text: except one gigantic lesbian. / Who is Conan O'Brien, and why is she so sad?
jokes.setup: Bucky saying he wandered the building and saw one gigantic lesbian
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's talking about Conan O'Brien
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - sets up expectation then reveals absurd misidentification of Conan. Perfect structure and timing.

jokes.text: So many different kind of buckets I wanted to own. Buckets.
jokes.setup: Tracy in purgatory talking about his bucket list
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation - wanting to own different buckets
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy logic - misunderstanding 'bucket list' as literal bucket ownership. Brilliantly stupid.

jokes.text: Black, Jewish, one eye, a white wife. It was a big tent back then, Tracy.
jokes.setup: Sammy Davis Jr. trying to convince Tracy to be Republican
jokes.punchline: Listing his own marginalized identities as proof of Republican inclusiveness
jokes.explanation: Smart historical reference using Sammy Davis Jr.'s actual intersectional identity. Good structure and character use.

jokes.text: Social conservative, fiscal liberal.
jokes.setup: Jack asking Dennis about his politics
jokes.punchline: Dennis's backwards political identity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis - having exactly the opposite political alignment from normal Republicans. Character-accurate contrarianism.

jokes.text: I almost joined the marines once.
jokes.setup: Dennis saying he can't help McCain because he's navy
jokes.punchline: His only military connection was almost joining the Marines
jokes.explanation: Classic Dennis - having strong military opinions based on minimal experience. Character-accurate overconfidence.

jokes.text: Warming your jeans in the morning? / That's right, and it feels good.
jokes.setup: Jenna guessing what Liz uses her oven for
jokes.punchline: Liz confirming and defending the weird habit
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz - weird domestic habit that's both sad and relatable. Jenna knowing it makes it better.

jokes.text: My cologne is distilled from the bilge water of Rupert Murdoch's yacht.
jokes.setup: Dennis saying Jack smells rich
jokes.punchline: Jack's absurdly specific and gross cologne source
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant Jack line - disgusting yet plausible for a rich executive. Perfect specificity and character integration.

jokes.text: Not thinking is what makes America great.
jokes.setup: Discussion about following instincts and not overthinking
jokes.punchline: Dennis's patriotic celebration of not thinking
jokes.explanation: Dennis accidentally making profound social commentary. His stupidity becomes accidentally insightful.

jokes.text: Like a killer whale going nuts on his trainer at sea world.
jokes.setup: Dennis saying when he grabs on, he doesn't let go
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to a killer whale attacking its trainer
jokes.explanation: Dark and specific comparison. Dennis choosing the most violent metaphor for relationship persistence is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Love is wearing makeup to bed and going downstairs to the Burger King to poop, and hiding alcohol in perfume bottles.
jokes.setup: Liz asking if being yourself isn't what love really is
jokes.punchline: Jenna's specific examples of romantic deception
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna - specific, neurotic examples of romantic deception. Each detail is more absurd than the last.

jokes.text: That's exactly what they say it's like when you freeze to death.
jokes.setup: Liz describing being with Dennis as numb, warm, and sleepy
jokes.punchline: Jenna comparing it to freezing to death
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark metaphor - perfectly describes bad relationships as literal death. Excellent structure and impact.

jokes.text: You wanted to do something private with another man, it wasn't gay. No, it was just two men celebrating each other's strength.
jokes.setup: Bucky talking about how men were men back then
jokes.punchline: His euphemistic description of homosexual activity
jokes.explanation: Period-accurate homophobic denial disguised as masculine bonding. Uncomfortable but character-consistent for old Hollywood.

jokes.text: That was never your problem.
jokes.setup: Dennis saying he used to overthink everything
jokes.punchline: Liz's dry correction that overthinking was never his issue
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan correction. Maximum economy - Liz deflating Dennis's self-analysis with brutal honesty.

jokes.text: That was never your problem.
jokes.setup: Dennis saying he used to overthink everything
jokes.punchline: Liz's deadpan correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan Liz correction. Economical and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: You know a stripper offered to give me a squeezer last night. A white stripper.
jokes.setup: Dennis defending himself after rejection
jokes.punchline: Adding 'A white stripper' as if that makes it more impressive
jokes.explanation: Dennis's casual racism in describing his 'romantic' options. Character-accurate but relies on offensive assumptions.

jokes.text: My fellow blackmericans-- / Can I just say black Americans? There's no such thing as blackmericans.
jokes.setup: Tracy starting his Republican commercial with 'blackmericans'
jokes.punchline: Jack immediately correcting the made-up word
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay with Tracy creating nonsense words and Jack's practical correction. Solid structure.

jokes.text: To build a 200 foot high wall to keep Mexico out. And he also hated the estate tax.
jokes.setup: Tracy describing Dr. King's dream
jokes.punchline: Attributing completely wrong political views to MLK
jokes.explanation: Tracy completely misrepresenting MLK's message for Republican talking points. Absurd but not exceptional.

jokes.text: To build a 200 foot high wall to keep Mexico out. And he also hated the estate tax.
jokes.setup: Tracy saying MLK had a dream they all share
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong about King's actual views
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist take on how politicians misrepresent MLK's legacy for their own purposes.

jokes.text: black people, don't vote. Just don't do it. In the amount of time it'd take for you to vote, you could play three games of pool.
jokes.setup: Tracy realizing black people will vote Democrat anyway
jokes.punchline: His solution is to tell them not to vote at all, with pool as alternative
jokes.explanation: Tracy's absurd voter suppression strategy with specific pool math. Character-accurate but politically uncomfortable.

jokes.text: I'm Tracy Jordan, and I improve this message.
jokes.setup: Tracy ending his political commercial
jokes.punchline: Saying 'improve' instead of 'approve'
jokes.explanation: Simple Tracy malapropism. 'Improve this message' suggests he makes everything better, which is very Tracy.

jokes.text: Winston dialed 911 with his beak and said fire, but only because he didn't know the word for rape.
jokes.setup: Jack introducing the 911 bird as Dennis's replacement
jokes.punchline: The dark revelation about what the bird actually witnessed
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark escalation - starts as cute animal story, becomes horrifying crime scene. Excellent structure.

jokes.text: That's why G.E.'s going to be introducing the pocket deep fryer.
jokes.setup: Jack sarcastically saying he bases business decisions on Liz's needs
jokes.punchline: The absurd product that would actually appeal to Liz
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurd product that's both ridiculous and something Liz would actually want. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Well, when we sobered up, we realized we'd eaten the peacock and we put the ham in pat weaver's office.
jokes.setup: Bucky telling another old NBC story
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions eating a peacock and misplacing ham
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist Bucky story - the casual delivery of eating a peacock is perfect.

jokes.text: If you're gonna make something beautiful, you gotta make a mess of it first.
jokes.setup: Kenneth criticizing Bucky for ruining television
jokes.punchline: Bucky's philosophical defense of creative chaos
jokes.explanation: Surprisingly wise words from the degenerate old TV star. Good character moment showing depth.

jokes.text: Now if you'll excuse me, I'm off to Chinatown to pick up some human growth hormones so that Mr. Jordan's tigers don't realize that he's aging
jokes.setup: Kenneth excusing himself from the conversation
jokes.punchline: The casual mention of Tracy's tigers and anti-aging concerns
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth casual mention of Tracy's insane lifestyle. The specificity of tigers recognizing aging is brilliant.

jokes.text: The mighty ducks are mad at me because I left the Stanley cup on a water taxi.
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining why his fame is fading
jokes.punchline: Consequences of his earlier Stanley Cup theft
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the Stanley Cup theft, with added absurdity of leaving it on water taxi. Dennis-level stupidity.

jokes.text: Boy, that's one mouthy sandwich girl, isn't it?
jokes.setup: Liz correcting Bucky about Kenneth and the Dateline situation
jokes.punchline: Bucky dismissing Liz with outdated terminology
jokes.explanation: Callback to 'sandwich girls' comment, now applied to Liz. Period-accurate sexism from the old TV star.

jokes.text: It's still daytime. And we're inside.
jokes.setup: Dennis setting a romantic scene saying 'night just like this'
jokes.punchline: Liz's practical correction of his attempt at atmosphere
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz deadpan deflation of Dennis's romantic moment. Maximum economy and character accuracy.

jokes.text: Yeah, it's totally biased against the Irish, you know.
jokes.setup: Liz asking if Dennis failed the firefighter exam
jokes.punchline: Dennis blaming anti-Irish bias instead of his own incompetence
jokes.explanation: Classic Dennis - never taking responsibility, always finding external blame. Character-consistent excuse-making.

jokes.text: If reality TV has taught us anything, it's that you can't keep people with no shame down.
jokes.setup: Liz trying to comfort Dennis about his future
jokes.punchline: Her backhanded encouragement based on reality TV principles
jokes.explanation: Excellent observational humor - reality TV as proof that shameless people succeed. Good structure and insight.

jokes.text: Ah, damn, it's the wrong track. It's stupid express.
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to recreate his heroic moment by jumping on tracks
jokes.punchline: His plan failing because he chose the wrong track
jokes.explanation: Dennis's incompetence ruining his attempt to recreate heroism. Physical comedy with character consistency.

jokes.text: You'll be back, Liz Lemon. You'll be back. / You'll be back, dummy.
jokes.setup: Dennis declaring Liz will return to him
jokes.punchline: Adding 'dummy' as his final insult
jokes.explanation: Classic Dennis ending - confident prediction followed by childish name-calling. Character-accurate but not exceptional.
